title: Form 25A — Order for Divorce
subtitle: Ontario Family Court — Family Law Rules (FLR 25A)
helpIntro: Form 25A is the actual divorce order that the judge signs. You prepare it in advance and submit it with your divorce application — if the judge approves your divorce, they sign this form and it becomes the legal divorce order. It is one of the most important documents in the process. Prepare it carefully.

parts.title: Court information
parts.subtitle: Step 1 of 5
parts.intro: Enter the court file details. This must exactly match your application.

fields.label: Courthouse
options: St. Catharines — Superior Court of Justice
Thunder Bay — Superior Court of Justice

fields.label: Court file number
Date of hearing (if known, otherwise leave blank)
fields.helpText: The clerk will fill this in if left blank.

parts.title: The parties
parts.subtitle: Step 2 of 5
parts.intro: Enter both parties' full legal names exactly as they appear on the marriage certificate.

fields.label: Applicant's full legal name
fields.helpText: Must match the marriage certificate exactly.

fields.label: Respondent's full legal name
fields.helpText: Must match the marriage certificate exactly.

fields.label: Applicant's date of birth
Respondent's date of birth

parts.title: The divorce order
parts.subtitle: Step 3 of 5
parts.intro: This section sets out the terms of the divorce order itself — what the judge will be signing.

fields.label: Date of marriage
Place of marriage (city and province/country)
Date of separation
When should the divorce take effect?
options: 31 days after the divorce order is made (standard — allows appeal period)
Immediately — I am asking the court to waive the 31-day waiting period
fields.helpText: The standard is 31 days. The divorce becomes final 31 days after the judge signs the order, unless an appeal is filed. The 31-day period can only be waived in very limited circumstances (e.g. one party is remarrying imminently).

fields.label: Reason for waiving the 31-day waiting period

conditional.showWhen: Immediately — I am asking the court to waive the 31-day waiting period

fields.placeholder: e.g. Both parties consent to the divorce being effective immediately as one party plans to remarry on [date].

parts.title: Additional orders (corollary relief)
parts.subtitle: Step 4 of 5
parts.intro: A divorce order can also include other orders — such as support or custody — if they are not already set out in a separate agreement or order. Only include items here that you need the divorce order itself to address.

fields.label: Should the divorce order include a child support term?
fields.helpText: Select 'No' if child support is already covered by a separation agreement or a separate court order.

fields.label: Child support term to include in the order
fields.placeholder: e.g. The Respondent shall pay to the Applicant child support for the children Alex Lance and Sam Lance in the amount of $1,200 per month commencing July 1, 2025, pursuant to the Child Support Guidelines.
fields.helpText: Write the exact wording you want the judge to sign. Be specific — include names, amounts, and start dates.

fields.label: Should the divorce order include a spousal support term?
fields.helpText: Select 'No' if spousal support is already covered by a separation agreement or separate order.

fields.label: Spousal support term to include in the order
fields.placeholder: e.g. The Respondent shall pay to the Applicant spousal support in the amount of $800 per month commencing July 1, 2025, for a period of 5 years.

fields.label: Should the divorce order include parenting / custody terms?
fields.helpText: Select 'No' if parenting is already addressed in a separation agreement or a prior court order.

fields.label: Parenting / custody term to include in the order
fields.placeholder: e.g. The children Alex Lance and Sam Lance shall reside primarily with the Applicant. The Respondent shall have parenting time as set out in the Separation Agreement dated March 15, 2025.

fields.label: No order as to costs (each party pays their own legal costs)
fields.helpText: In uncontested divorces, courts typically make no order as to costs. Check this box if that is your intention.

fields.label: If a costs order is requested, describe it
fields.placeholder: e.g. The Respondent shall pay costs of this proceeding to the Applicant fixed at $X.

parts.title: Review and confirmation
parts.subtitle: Step 5 of 5
parts.intro: Review the draft Order for Divorce carefully. The judge will sign exactly what you have prepared here. Once signed, it is a binding court order.

fields.label: Is the applicant resuming a former surname after the divorce?
fields.helpText: You can resume a name you used before the marriage. This can be included in the divorce order itself.

fields.label: Applicant's former surname to be resumed
fields.helpText: You will use the divorce order as proof of name change — no other process required.

fields.label: Is this an uncontested (joint or sole) divorce where both parties agree?
fields.helpText: If the other party is contesting the divorce, the order terms will be determined by the court at a hearing.

fields.label: I confirm this Order for Divorce accurately reflects what I am asking the court to order.
